title: Rust Fast Path
description: Trivial Rust change (< 20 lines). modum check, self-review, PR. Stops at PR — user handles CI, review, merge.

tasks.title: Verify branch base
tasks.description: Fetch origin/main. Create branch from origin/main (NOT upstream/main — the two remotes have diverged; upstream/main has commits not in origin/main). Verify with: `git diff origin/main --stat` should show ONLY the intended changed files. If it shows hundreds of files, you're based on the wrong branch — start fresh from origin/main.
acceptance_criteria: git diff origin/main --stat shows only intended files

tasks.title: modum check
tasks.description: Run `modum check --mode error` on the crate root. Fix any violations before proceeding. This is the preflight check since we can't compile on this machine.

tasks.title: Self-review diff
tasks.description:
Read through the ENTIRE diff (`git diff origin/main`) line by line. Check for:
- Logic errors: Does the control flow handle all edge cases? (early returns, None/Err paths, fallthroughs, type conversions)
- Unchanged call sites: Did you update ALL callers of a refactored function? Grep for old signatures — this is the most common failure pattern
- Message correctness: Do user-facing strings reference the right flags, paths, and commands?
- Consistency: Are variable names, function signatures, and arg ordering consistent across the change?
- Edge cases: What happens when a dependency is missing, a subprocess fails, a config is empty?

tasks.title: Push + PR
tasks.description:
Verify branch is NEW: `git branch -r | grep <branch-name>`. If it already exists on any remote, pick a different name (e.g. `-v2`). Never push to an existing branch unless you specifically mean to update it.

Push branch to upstream (NOT origin): `git push upstream <branch>`.

Create PR: `gh pr create --repo adolfousier/stemcell --head leshchenko1979:<branch>`

Verify PR diff: `gh pr diff --name-only` should match expected files. If it doesn't, close the PR and fix.
